response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with genuine distributed-systems depth (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic) and a solid testing/TDD culture. However, this role is fundamentally a C++ technical-leadership position requiring 8 years of C++, and the resume shows only roughly six months of professional C/C++ (National Instruments, 2014–2015) plus an academic thesis. The primary stack throughout the career is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not C++. The hard minimum on C++ is missed by a wide margin, and formal cloud/DevOps technical-leadership experience is limited, making this a poor fit despite the engineering breadth.
strengths: Real large-scale distributed-systems experience (millions of req/sec, low-latency, Kubernetes)
Strong testing, TDD, and CI/CD craftsmanship background
Broad engineering range plus mentoring/teaching and founder-level ownership
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum (only ~6 months professional C/C++)
Core stack is TypeScript/Node and DLang, not the role's C++ focus
Limited formal cloud/DevOps technical-leadership and architecture-ownership experience
